title: Gold vs Bitcoin: collapse insurance?
question: When equities collapse, does gold protect — and is Bitcoin "digital gold" or a risk asset?
finding: Bitcoin is a risk asset, not digital gold. Across 8 SPY selloffs (≥8%) gold beat the market in 7/8 (median −3%) and Bitcoin fell worse in 7/8 (median −29%) — both significant (sign p=0.035; Wilcoxon p=0.008/0.012). Bitcoin's daily beta to SPY is 0.94 (1.68 in the worst-5% tail, HAC t=7.2/3.1) and its 90-day SPY correlation rose 0.08→0.37, the later half higher in 107/107 split points (trend t=7.2). Gold is the better cushion but not an always-up haven (positive tail beta +0.37).
